method: Each model answers the same DRACO corpus question (gut microbiome ↔ mental health, 600–900 word citation-grounded dossier) via the live OpenRouter API. Each dossier scored 0–100 by 3 BLIND judges (median = self-consistency). Cost = (total_tokens / 1e6) × blended USD/Mtok from packages/bench/src/draco/cost-efficiency.ts. All numbers are real OpenRouter usage + real judge output — nothing fabricated.
judge_rubric: coverage (gut-brain axis, serotonin, depression, probiotics, causation-vs-correlation) + citations (plausible author+year+venue, penalize fabricated) + nuance (consensus + 2 strongest dissents).
note: First pass used max_tokens=1600; gpt-5, gpt-5-mini (reasoning models) and gemini-2.5-pro were truncated (reasoning consumed the budget / cut off). Re-run at 8000 tokens — these three rows reflect the complete dossiers.

dominated.anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5: 76@$0.00468 dominated by gemini-2.5-flash 82@$0.00146 (cheaper AND higher quality)
dominated.anthropic/claude-opus-4: 76@$0.06138 dominated by every other model — worst quality/$ despite highest price

headline.cheap_beats_frontier: google/gemini-2.5-flash (cheap, $1/Mtok) scores 82 > anthropic/claude-opus-4 (frontier, $45/Mtok) at 76 — HIGHER judged quality at 1/42 the cost, and 4× faster (9.4s vs 37.2s).
headline.quality_per_dollar: gemini-2.5-flash = 56,280 quality/$ vs opus-4 1,238 (45.5×) and gpt-5 1,319 (42.7×).
headline.diminishing_returns: gpt-5's top quality (93) costs 48× more per dossier than gemini-2.5-flash's 82 — a +13% quality gain for +4,739% cost.
